html:
[1686]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 情鬼类 - A specific category of beings or phenomena that are emotionally driven or associated with spirits.
  • 比翼凤 - A mythical bird known for its dual nature, symbolizing love and fidelity. The phrase '比翼' (bǐ yì) means 'sharing the same wing,' emphasizing their inseparability.
  • 通宿命 - Means 'to understand past lives' or 'to have knowledge of previous existences,' implying a supernatural ability.
  • 死而复生 - Means 'to die and be reborn,' referring to the phoenix-like quality of the bird.
  • 集于长桐之上 - Means 'gathered on the tall mulberry tree,' describing a specific location where the birds were seen.
  • 双头鸟 - Means 'two-headed bird,' a literal description of the bird's appearance.
  • 不祥 - Means 'unlucky' or 'inauspicious,' indicating the negative perception of the bird during the time of King Zhou.
  • 并配之瑞也 - Means 'a sign of good fortune in pairing,' suggesting that the bird's presence was a positive omen during the reigns of King Wen and King Wu.
  • 杳杳 - Means 'distant' or 'far away,' conveying a sense of longing.
  • 绵绵 - Means 'enduring' or 'long-lasting,' emphasizing the bird's presence and influence.
  • 悠悠我思 - Means 'my thoughts wander,' expressing deep contemplation.
  • 永与愿违 - Means 'always contrary to my wishes,' indicating a sense of frustration or unfulfilled desire.
  • 万劫无期 - Means 'an endless cycle of rebirth,' referring to the concept of reincarnation.
  • 何时来飞 - Means 'when will you return,' expressing a plea for the bird's return.
  • 忽然而生 - Means 'suddenly appeared,' describing the unexpected reappearance of the bird.
  • 径入云中 - Means 'directly into the clouds,' indicating the bird's ascent into the sky.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 比翼凤 - A mythical bird symbolizing love and fidelity, often mentioned in Chinese literature and folklore.
  • 纣时 - Refers to the reign of King Zhou, the last ruler of the Shang Dynasty, known for his tyranny.
  • 文、武兴 - Refers to the reigns of King Wen and King Wu of Zhou, who overthrew the Shang Dynasty and established the Zhou Dynasty.
  • 长桐 - Refers to a tall mulberry tree, a significant location in the story.
  • 卫罗国王 - A specific historical figure, the king of the Western Wei Luo kingdom.
  • 配英 - The name of the king's daughter, a character in the story.
  • 洞玄本行经 - A Taoist scripture, providing the source of the second story.

Proper Nouns

  • 情鬼类 - A specific category of beings or phenomena.
  • 比翼凤 - A mythical bird.
  • 野君 - The male bird's name.
  • 观讳 - The female bird's name.
  • 长离 - The combined name of the birds.
  • 纣 - King Zhou, the last ruler of the Shang Dynasty.
  • 文、武 - King Wen and King Wu of Zhou, rulers of the Zhou Dynasty.
  • 长桐 - A tall mulberry tree.
  • 卫罗国王 - The king of the Western Wei Luo kingdom.
  • 配英 - The king's daughter.
  • 灵凤 - The mythical bird.
  • 皇妃 - The daughter born after the bird's reappearance.
  • 洞玄本行经 - A Taoist scripture.

[1687]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 欲其鳴 - Literally 'to make it sing,' referring to the king's desire to hear the phoenix sing.
  • 飾金繁，饗珍羞 - Adorned with gold and fine food, describing the lavish treatment of the phoenix.
  • 對之愈戚 - Became more sorrowful when facing it, indicating the phoenix's deep sadness.
  • 三年不鳴 - Did not sing for three years, emphasizing the prolonged period of silence.
  • 交頸、頡頏、撫翼 - Necking, nodding, and caressing wings, describing the intimate and affectionate behavior of the cranes.
  • 聞奏鐘磬，翩然共舞 - When they hear the sound of bells and chimes, they dance gracefully together, depicting the cranes' graceful movements in response to music.
  • 婉轉低昂，妙契弦節 - Winding up and down, perfectly in tune with the rhythm, describing the cranes' harmonious and rhythmic movements.
  • 素素 - The name of the white crane, which is a proper noun but also carries a symbolic meaning of purity and elegance.
  • 昔王母青鸞，紹蘭燕子 - The Queen Mother's blue phoenix and the swallow of the Shao family, historical and mythological references to other birds known for their communication abilities.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 罽賓國 - Refers to the country of Khotan, located in present-day Xinjiang, China, known for its rich cultural heritage.
  • 湘東王 - Refers to Xiao Ziliang, a prince of the Liang Dynasty, known for his love of literature and art.
  • 新楊太守鄭裒 - Refers to Zheng Piu, the governor of Xinyang, who sent the male crane to the bamboo forest hall.
  • 異苑 - A collection of strange and unusual stories compiled by Liu Yiqing, a scholar of the Southern Dynasties.
  • 王母青鸞 - Refers to the blue phoenix of the Queen Mother of the West, a figure from Chinese mythology known for her magical and supernatural abilities.
  • 紹蘭燕子 - Refers to the swallow of the Shao family, a legendary bird known for its ability to deliver messages over long distances.

Proper Nouns

  • 罽賓國 - Khotan, a historical kingdom in Xinjiang, China.
  • 湘東王 - Xiao Ziliang, a prince of the Liang Dynasty.
  • 新楊太守鄭裒 - Zheng Piu, the governor of Xinyang.
  • 異苑 - A collection of strange and unusual stories.
  • 王母青鸞 - The blue phoenix of the Queen Mother of the West.
  • 紹蘭燕子 - The swallow of the Shao family.
  • 素素 - The name of the white crane, owned by Chao Cai.

[1688]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 尪然待尽 - Describes a state of extreme weakness or emaciation, 'awaiting death'; conveys a vivid image of someone on the brink of death.
  • 挥使大怒，欲掴其面 - Describes the husband's strong emotional reaction, 'slap his face'; conveys a sense of anger and frustration.
  • 屏后趋出止之 - Describes the wife's quick and decisive action, 'hurriedly came out and stopped him'; emphasizes her role in mediating the situation.
  • 终不以淫欲为事 - Emphasizes the spiritual purity of the deity, 'never do things for sexual desire'; highlights the moral and ethical stance of the deity.
  • 辅以汤药饮食 - Describes the medical care provided, 'supported by medicinal soups and food'; emphasizes the holistic approach to healing.
  • 痼疾顿除 - Indicates a sudden and complete recovery, 'chronic illness suddenly cured'; conveys a dramatic and miraculous healing.
  • 乘石鹤而去 - Describes the deity's departure in a supernatural manner, 'ride the stone crane and depart'; emphasizes the mystical and otherworldly nature of the deity.
  • 旧有两石鹤 - Introduces the historical presence of the stone cranes, 'had previously two stone cranes'; provides context for the stone cranes' significance.
  • 乘其雄者上升 - Describes the deity's ascent, 'ride the male one and ascend'; emphasizes the spiritual and mystical journey.
  • 中夜悲啼 - Describes the female stone crane's emotional response, 'sadly weep at midnight'; conveys a sense of sorrow and loss.
  • 争来击落其喙 - Describes the people's reaction to the stone crane's weeping, 'rushed to knock off its beak'; emphasizes the emotional impact on the audience.
  • 秦吉了 - A type of bird known for its ability to mimic human speech, often featured in Chinese literature; adds a layer of cultural richness to the narrative.
  • 则天问曰 - Introduces Empress Wu Zetian's questioning, 'asked'; sets the stage for the empress's interaction with the birds.
  • 卿何故藏一鸟不进 - Describes the empress's accusation, 'why hide a bird and not present it'; highlights the empress's authority and curiosity.
  • 景阳叩头谢罪 - Describes Duan Jingyang's response to the empress, 'knelt and bowed and apologized'; emphasizes his respect and compliance.
  • 则天不罪也 - Concludes the empress's reaction, 'did not punish'; emphasizes the empress's mercy and the resolution of the conflict.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 挥使 - Refers to the husband of the sick daughter, a common term for a man in a family setting; provides context for the family dynamics.
  • 细君 - Refers to the wife of the sick daughter, a term used to address a woman respectfully; provides context for the family dynamics.
  • 蓬头 - Likely a reference to a deity or spiritual figure, given the context of miraculous healing; emphasizes the supernatural element.
  • 西川鹤鸣观 - Refers to a specific location, likely a temple or observatory in the western region of China; provides historical and geographical context.
  • 则天 - Refers to Empress Wu Zetian, one of the most powerful and influential figures in Chinese history, who ruled during the Tang Dynasty; emphasizes the historical significance.
  • 刘景阳 - A historical figure, likely a government official or traveler, who brought the birds to the capital; provides context for the narrative.

Proper Nouns

  • 挥使 - The husband of the sick daughter; a key character in the narrative.
  • 细君 - The wife of the sick daughter; a key character in the narrative.
  • 蓬头 - A deity or spiritual figure; a central figure in the narrative.
  • 西川鹤鸣观 - A specific location, likely a temple or observatory; provides historical and geographical context.
  • 秦吉了 - A type of bird known for its ability to mimic human speech; adds a layer of cultural richness to the narrative.
  • 则天 - Empress Wu Zetian, a powerful and influential figure in Chinese history; a key historical figure in the narrative.
  • 刘景阳 - A historical figure, likely a government official or traveler; a key character in the narrative.

[1689]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鸳鸯 - A type of duck known for its monogamous mating habits, often used in literature to symbolize fidelity.
  • 鹰攫 - Means 'hawk snatches' or 'hawk grabs,' describing the action of the hawk capturing the bird.
  • 其偶悲鸣 - Literally 'its mate mourns,' indicating the emotional response of the captured bird's partner.
  • 上下不去 - Means 'continues to fly up and down without leaving,' describing the persistent behavior of the bird.
  • 惕然问 - Means 'asked with concern' or 'asked with worry,' indicating the emperor's thoughtful inquiry.
  • 资识性情 - Means 'nature, character, and temperament,' referring to the inherent qualities of the birds.
  • 鸷鸟 - Refers to predatory birds, particularly those that are fierce and powerful.
  • 下诏禁断 - Means 'issued an edict to prohibit,' indicating the emperor's decision to ban the keeping of such birds.
  • 捕得二雁 - Means 'caught two geese,' describing the action of the fisherman.
  • 空中盘旋 - Means 'flew in circles in the air,' describing the movement of the escaped goose.
  • 哀鸣良久 - Means 'mourned for a long time,' indicating the prolonged expression of grief.
  • 投地死 - Means 'fell to the ground and died,' describing the final outcome of the goose.
  • 以金赎得 - Means 'redeemed with gold,' indicating the act of paying to save the geese.
  • 瘗汾水旁 - Means 'buried beside the Fen River,' describing the burial location.
  • 垒石为识 - Means 'built a stone marker,' indicating the creation of a memorial.
  • 摸鱼儿 - A type of poem, often used to express deep emotions.
  • 问世间、情是何物 - A rhetorical question meaning 'What is the nature of love in the world?'
  • 直教生死相许 - Means 'it makes them willing to die for each other,' emphasizing the intensity of the relationship.
  • 天南地北双飞客 - Means 'two flying companions from the far south and north,' describing the geese's journey.
  • 老翅几回寒暑 - Means 'old wings have endured many cold and hot seasons,' indicating the geese's long life.
  • 欢乐趣 - Means 'joy and pleasure,' describing the happiness shared by the geese.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元魏显宗延兴三年 - Refers to the third year of the Xianzong era of the Northern Wei dynasty, a specific historical period.
  • 元好问 - A famous poet of the Jin dynasty, known for his contributions to Chinese literature.
  • 金人 - Refers to the Jin dynasty, which ruled parts of northern China.
  • 并州 - A historical region in northern China, where the Jin dynasty was based.
  • 高邮湖 - A lake in Jiangsu Province, China.
  • 汾水 - The Fen River, a major river in Shanxi Province, China.
  • 尔雅 - An ancient Chinese dictionary and encyclopedia, often cited for its definitions and explanations of classical terms.
  • 比翼鸟 - A mythical bird that flies with another of its kind, often used to symbolize a close and inseparable relationship.
  • 鹣鹣 - Another name for the mythical bird, emphasizing its unique characteristics.
  • 词家 - Refers to poets or writers of classical Chinese poetry.
  • 雁 - Geese, often used in literature to symbolize loyalty and fidelity.

Proper Nouns

  • 元魏显宗延兴三年 - The specific historical period.
  • 元好问 - The poet.
  • 并州 - The historical region.
  • 高邮湖 - The lake.
  • 汾水 - The river.
  • 尔雅 - The classical reference.
  • 比翼鸟 - The mythical bird.
  • 鹣鹣 - Another name for the mythical bird.
  • 雁 - The geese.
  • 摸鱼儿 - The type of poem.

[1690]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 离别苦 - Common expression meaning 'the pain of parting.'
  • 痴儿女 - Literally 'foolish children' but refers to lovers or couples deeply attached to each other.
  • 渺万里层云，千山暮雪，只影向谁去 - Poetic phrase describing vastness and desolation, emphasizing the loneliness of the subject.
  • 横汾路 - Refers to a specific road or path, possibly a metaphor for a journey or life path.
  • 寂寞当年箫鼓 - Describes the desolation and silence of a place once filled with music and celebration.
  • 荒烟依旧平楚 - Poetic description of a desolate landscape, '平楚' refers to a flat, open area covered with trees.
  • 招魂楚些嗟何及 - Reference to the ancient Chinese ritual of calling back the soul, '楚些' is a form of lamentation.
  • 山鬼暗啼风雨 - Describes eerie sounds of nature, possibly referring to spirits or supernatural beings.
  • 天地妒 - Metaphorical expression suggesting that the heavens and earth envy the beauty or fate of the subject.
  • 莺儿燕子俱黄土 - Poetic way of saying that even birds will eventually die and turn to dust.
  • 狂歌痛饮 - Describes a wild, unrestrained celebration or expression of emotion.
  • 雁丘 - Place or monument dedicated to the memory of a pair of cranes that died together.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 栾城李仁卿治和云 - Refers to Li Renqing, a person from Luancheng, who composed a poem in response to the original text.
  • 雁双双、正分汾水 - Describes a pair of cranes separated by the Fen River, symbolizing love and separation.
  • 天长地久相思债 - Metaphor for the enduring nature of love and the debt of longing.
  • 塞北江南 - Refers to the northern and southern regions of China, often used to describe contrasting landscapes or experiences.
  • 风嘹月唳 - Describes the sounds of the wind and the moon, often used in poetry to evoke a sense of loneliness or longing.
  • 桐江秋影今何在 - Refers to the shadow of autumn on the Tong River, a place associated with the poet Xie Lingyun.
  • 王嫱青冢真娘墓 - Refers to the tombs of Wang Qiang (a famous beauty), Qingzhong (a place), and Zhennang (another place), all associated with historical or legendary figures.

Proper Nouns

  • 雁丘 - Place or monument dedicated to the memory of a pair of cranes that died together.
  • 栾城 - Place name, likely referring to a city or region in China.
  • 李仁卿 - A person, likely a poet or writer from Luancheng.
  • 汾水 - The Fen River, a river in China.
  • 桐江 - The Tong River, a river in China.
  • 王嫱 - A historical or legendary figure, often associated with beauty.
  • 青冢 - A place name, possibly referring to a tomb or burial site.
  • 真娘墓 - A place name, possibly referring to a tomb or burial site.

[1691]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 民舍除烟缭绕，祓除不祥 - Household ridding itself of bad luck through smoke.
  • 一雁偶为烟触而下 - Geese accidentally hit by the smoke and fell down.
  • 其家直以为不祥也，烹之 - Household thought it was a bad omen and cooked it.
  • 明日，一雁飞鸣屋顶，数日亦坠而死 - Next day, another goose flew and called on the roof and also fell and died after several days.
  • 弘治间，河南虞人获一雌雁 - During the Hongzhi era, a gamekeeper in Henan captured a female goose.
  • 缚其羽，蓄诸场圃，以媒他雁 - Tied its feathers, kept it in a field, and used it to attract other geese.
  • 至次年来宾时，其雄者与群雁飞鸣而过 - When the next year arrived, the male goose flew and called along with the flock of geese.
  • 雌认其声，仰空号鸣 - Female recognized the sound and called out into the sky.
  • 雄亦认其声，遂飞落圃中 - Male also recognized the sound and then flew down into the field.
  • 交颈悲号，其声呜呜，若相哀诉者 - Called out to each other with necks entwined, mournful cries seemed like they were lamenting to each other.
  • 如此者三四次，知终不能飞去，乃共啮颈蹂蹴，遂相愤触而死 - This happened several times, realized they could not fly away, bit each other's necks and trampled each other, died from mutual anger and collision.
  • 雁为禽类，而且有恩义 - Geese are birds and also have feelings of gratitude and righteousness.
  • 人之夫妇相抛弃而不顾者，何独无人心哉 - People who abandon and ignore their spouses, how can they lack human compassion?
  • 燕 - Swallows, often associated with fidelity and loyalty.
  • 襄阳卫敬瑜早丧 - Xiangyang's Wei Jingyu died young.
  • 其妻，霸陵王整妹也 - His wife was the sister of Wang Zheng from Balin.
  • 年十六，父母舅姑咸欲嫁之 - She was sixteen years old, and her parents and in-laws all wanted to marry her off.
  • 誓而不许，截耳置盘中为誓，乃止 - Swore not to agree, cut off her ear and placed it in a dish as a vow, they then stopped.
  • 户有燕巢，常双来去 - House had a swallow nest, often came and went together.
  • 因以缕志其足 - Tied a thread to its foot.
  • 明年复来，孤飞如故，犹带前缕 - Next year it came back, still flew alone, still had the thread from before.
  • 女作诗曰 - Woman wrote a poem saying.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 民舍 - Refers to a common household.
  • 祓除不祥 - Traditional practice of ridding oneself of bad luck or misfortune.
  • 弘治间 - Refers to the Hongzhi era (1488-1505) during the Ming Dynasty.
  • 河南 - Henan province in China.
  • 虞人 - A gamekeeper or hunter.
  • 场圃 - A field or enclosure for keeping animals.
  • 媒他雁 - Using one animal to attract another.
  • 襄阳 - Xiangyang, a city in Hubei province.
  • 霸陵 - Balin, a place name.
  • 燕 - Swallows, often associated with fidelity and loyalty in Chinese culture.
  • 卫敬瑜 - A historical figure.
  • 王整 - Another historical figure.
  • 父母舅姑 - Refers to parents and in-laws.
  • 誓而不许 - Swearing an oath not to agree.
  • 截耳置盘中为誓 - Cutting off an ear and placing it in a dish as a vow, a form of extreme self-sacrifice.
  • 户有燕巢 - Having a swallow nest in the house, symbolizing harmony and fidelity.
  • 孤飞 - Flying alone, often seen as a sign of loneliness or distress.

[1692]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 昔年无偶去，今春犹独归 - Poetic expression describing the loneliness of the swallow, indicating that in past years it flew away without a mate and now returns alone in spring.
  • 故人恩既重，不忍复双飞 - Metaphorical expression conveying the deep bond between the swallow and its former companion, suggesting that the swallow cannot bear to fly together again due to the weight of their past relationship.
  • 自尔春来秋去，凡六七年 - Temporal expression indicating that the swallow has been coming and going for six or seven years since the separation.
  • 燕绕舍哀鸣 - Descriptive phrase showing the swallow circling the house and crying out in sorrow.
  • 人告之葬处，即飞就墓，哀鸣不食而死 - Narrative sequence describing the swallow's reaction to being told about its companion's burial place, flying to the grave, mourning, and eventually dying.
  • 号曰"燕冢" - Naming convention indicating that the grave of the swallow was called "Swallow Tomb."
  • 一说 - Introductory phrase indicating that an alternative version of the story is about to be presented.
  • 姚玉京嫁襄州小吏卫敬瑜 - Narrative introduction to the alternative story, stating that Yao Yujing married a minor official named Wei Jingyu in Xiangzhou.
  • 卫溺死 - Tragic event indicating that Wei drowned.
  • 玉京守志 - Descriptive phrase indicating that Yao Yujing remained faithful to her deceased husband.
  • 常有双燕巢梁间 - Descriptive phrase indicating that there were always two swallows nesting in the eaves.
  • 为鸷鸟所获 - Narrative event indicating that the swallows were captured by a predator.
  • 其一孤飞悲鸣，徘徊至秋，翔集玉京之臂，如告别然 - Descriptive sequence indicating that one of the swallows flew alone, cried out, and lingered until autumn, finally alighting on Yao Yujing's arm as if to bid farewell.
  • 玉京以红缕系其足，曰："新春复来为吾侣也。" - Narrative sequence indicating that Yao Yujing tied a red thread to the swallow's foot and promised that it would return in the new year.
  • 明年果至 - Temporal expression indicating that the swallow did indeed return the following year.
  • 玉京为诗云云 - Narrative sequence indicating that Yao Yujing wrote a poem about the event.
  • 后玉京卒 - Temporal expression indicating that Yao Yujing eventually died.
  • 燕复来，周回悲鸣 - Descriptive phrase indicating that the swallow returned and flew around, crying out in sorrow.
  • 家人语曰："玉京死矣，坟在南郭。" - Narrative sequence indicating that the family told the swallow about Yao Yujing's death and the location of her grave.
  • 燕至坟所亦死 - Narrative sequence indicating that the swallow died at the grave.
  • 每风清月皎，或见玉京与燕同游灞水之上焉 - Descriptive phrase indicating that sometimes, under clear skies and bright moons, people saw Yao Yujing and the swallow playing together on the banks of the Bashi River.
  • 或云：玉京即王氏乳名；加姚者，从母姓也 - Note indicating that Yao Yujing was actually named Wang at birth, and her surname Yao was adopted from her mother's family.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 燕 - Refers to the swallow, a bird often associated with fidelity and loyalty in Chinese culture.
  • 燕冢 - The "Swallow Tomb," a grave site named after the story of the swallows.
  • 襄州 - A historical region in present-day Hubei Province, China.
  • 小吏 - Refers to a minor official, indicating a lower-ranking government position.
  • 卫敬瑜 - The name of the minor official Yao Yujing married.
  • 姚玉京 - The name of the woman in the alternative story.
  • 王氏 - Indicates that Yao Yujing's original surname was Wang.
  • 灞水 - The Bashi River, a river in Shaanxi Province, China.
  • 南郭 - Refers to the southern outskirts of a city.
  • 《南史》 - A historical text compiled during the Tang Dynasty, covering the history of the Southern Dynasties.
  • 唐李公佐 - The name of the author who wrote the "Swallow Girl's Tomb" story.
  • 《燕女坟记》 - The title of the story written by Li Gongzuo.

Proper Nouns

  • 燕 - The swallow.
  • 燕冢 - The Swallow Tomb.
  • 襄州 - Xiangzhou.
  • 小吏 - Minor official.
  • 卫敬瑜 - Wei Jingyu.
  • 姚玉京 - Yao Yujing.
  • 王氏 - Wang.
  • 灞水 - Bashi River.
  • 南郭 - Southern outskirts.
  • 《南史》 - Nanshi (History of the Southern Dynasties).
  • 唐李公佐 - Tang Dynasty Li Gongzuo.
  • 《燕女坟记》 - Yannü Fuming Ji (Record of the Swallow Girl's Tomb).

[1693]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 元元貞二年 - Specific year in the Yuanyuan Zhen era.
  • 雙燕巢於燕人楊湯佐之宅 - Literal: 'a pair of swallows nesting in the house of a Yan person named Yang Tangzuo.' Here, '燕人' likely refers to someone from the Yan region, but it could also be a homophone for '燕子' (swallows).
  • 舉其燈照蝎 - Literal: 'lifting the lamp to illuminate the scorpion,' indicating a sudden, bright light.
  • 其雄驚墜，為貓所食 - Literal: 'the male was startled and fell, and was eaten by a cat.'
  • 雌彷徨悲鳴不已 - Literal: 'the female wandered and mourned continuously.'
  • 朝夕守巢 - Literal: 'guarded the nest day and night.'
  • 諸雛成翼而去 - Literal: 'the young ones grew wings and flew away.'
  • 明年，雌獨來復巢其處 - Literal: 'the next year, the female came alone to rebuild the nest in the same place.'
